     The Numbers Business Report

His Only Son (2023)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $12,084,084Details
International Box Office $1,422,631
Worldwide Box Office $13,506,715
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Abraham is commanded by God to sacrifice his son Isaac on the mountain of Moriah. While traveling to the place of the sacrifice, alongside Isaac and two servants, Abraham is flooded with vivid memories from the years he and Sarah spent longing for the son they were promised—the son he must now lay upon the altar.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$5,501,070 (45.5% of total gross)
Legs:2.20 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:89.5% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$250,000 (worldwide box office is 54.0 times production budget)
Theater counts:1,920 opening theaters/1,930 max. theaters, 2.5 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $13,210,753

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: March 31st, 2023 (Wide) by Angel Studios
International Releases: March 30th, 2023 (Limited) (Australia)
March 30th, 2023 (Limited) (New Zealand)
June 23rd, 2023 (Limited), released as Su Único Hijo (Spain)
June 28th, 2023 (Limited), released as His Only Son (하나님의 마음) (South Korea)
August 17th, 2023 (Limited), released as Su Único Hijo (Mexico)
Video Release: May 9th, 2023 by Angel Studios
MPA Rating: PG-13 for thematic content and some violence.
(Rating bulletin 2767 (cert #54243), 1/25/2023)
Running Time: 106 minutes
Social setting: Bible Epic
Subgenre: Faith-Based Drama
Target audience: Faith-Based Film
Source:Based on Religious Text
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: RockBridge Productions, Commissioned Pictures
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Abraham is commanded by God to sacrifice his son Isaac on the mountain of Moriah. While traveling to the place of the sacrifice, alongside Isaac and two servants, Abraham is flooded with vivid memories from the years he and Sarah spent longing for the son they were promised—the son he must now lay upon the altar.
